safe_to_use_for: testing Evidence Pack v1 traceability on a second TWIS article draft
not_safe_to_use_for: proving tariff or trade policy effects
proving consumer price effects without external sources
publishing a full TWIS article evidence pack without further checking
limits: no fact-checking of trade or pricing claims beyond article-framing level

source_role: Reuters report on EU parcel duty decision
safe_to_use_for: supporting the specific EU low-value parcel duty point
recording the reported date and amount
not_safe_to_use_for: proving the full article is publication-ready
limits: primary-source checking still recommended before publication

safe_to_use_for: supporting the official EU context on low-value consignments
recording the 150 euro threshold and customs reform framing
not_safe_to_use_for: proving the specific 3 euro July 2026 duty by itself
limits: supports context and policy framing rather than the later Reuters-specific duty amount and start date

safe_to_use_for: supporting the general point that price rises on cheaper goods can hit poorer households harder
recording IFS cheapflation findings as reported by the Guardian
not_safe_to_use_for: proving the specific consumer impact of the EU parcel duty

source_role: Reuters platform-use context source on Shein and Temu shoppers
safe_to_use_for: supporting the general point that Shein and Temu appeal to lower-income bargain-seeking shoppers
showing platform-use relevance to low-cost consumer goods

proving measured harm from EU customs changes
